channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A new mayor takes office in southern Mexico after his predecessor was beheaded
item.description: CHILPANCINGO, Mexico (AP) — A new mayor was sworn in Thursday in a city in southern Mexico where his predecessor was killed and beheaded less than a week after taking office.
item.dc:creator: ALEJANDRINO GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy city-hops across Europe, promoting 'victory plan' to allies.
item.description: ROME (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was city-hopping across Europe on Thursday to promote a “victory plan” that he said “aims to create the right conditions for a just end to the war" against Russia, detailing the proposals to European allies after a scheduled summit involving U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexico sends 660 soldiers, National Guard to protect lime growers suffering extortion by cartels
item.description: Mexico has sent 660 soldiers and militarized National Guard officers to the western state of Michoacan to protect lime growers who complained they were suffering extortion demands by cartels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rome mayor responds after Macron says he'll fight to keep 'Emily in Paris' in France: 'Netflix does not take orders from heads of state'
item.description: "Emily in Paris" is sparking a très dramatique back-and-forth between the French president and the mayor of Rome, who are battling about the Netflix series potentially bidding adieu to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: After a 2-year investigation, South African president won't be charged over $580K hidden in a couch
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa won't face criminal charges over a hidden cash scandal that was revealed more than two years ago and led to an investigation by a special police unit, prosecutors said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Donald Trump rolls out tax breaks, but no specifics, for overseas citizens and auto buyers
item.description: Donald Trump is rolling out more plans for tax breaks without offering details on how they would work and the impact they would have on the federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN-backed experts say Israel is destroying Gaza's health sector and both sides have tortured people
item.description: A U.N. commission on Thursday accused Israel of destroying Gaza's health care system through “relentless and deliberate attacks” in its yearlong war with Hamas and said that Palestinian prisoners and Israeli hostages have been tortured and sexually abused.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cambodian fishermen turn to raising eels as Tonle Sap lake runs out of fish
item.description: KAMPONG PHLUK, Cambodia (AP) — Em Phat, 53, studies his eel tanks with the intensity of a man gambling with his livelihood.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL and ANTON L. DELGADO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China defiant over South China Sea skirmishes in ASEAN talks and blames meddling by foreign forces
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — Southeast Asian leaders stepped up pressure on China to respect international law following clashes in the disputed South China Sea, but Chinese Premier Li Qiang was defiant during annual summit talks on Thursday as he blamed “external forces” for interfering in regional affai
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG AND JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump-Putin ties face fresh scrutiny with Woodward book
item.description: Less than a month before the U.S. presidential election, former President Trump is facing fresh scrutiny on his relationship with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: At least 22 killed in airstrikes in central Beirut, with Israel also firing on UN peacekeepers
item.description: Lebanon’s health ministry says at least 22 people have been killed and 117 have been wounded in Israeli airstrikes that hit two different areas in central Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: With roads often blocked by rebels in Congo, boats — the only alternative — have become death traps
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The overcrowded boat that capsized in eastern Congo last week killed eight members of Serge Nzonga’s family along with 70 others. Days later, he was back on the same route that claimed their lives in yet another boat lacking safety measures.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: A series of attacks by Yemen’s Houthi rebels have targeted a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkey's navy starts to evacuate its citizens from Beirut as Israel battles Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Over 2,000 Turkish citizens and some foreign nationals started boarding a Turkish military ship late Wednesday that brought in aid and will take them out of a country being hit by Israeli airstrikes targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Biden, Netanyahu speak ahead of expected retaliation against Iran
item.description: President Biden spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday for the first time in weeks, amid heightened tensions between the leaders as Israel calibrates its expected retaliatory strike against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID-19 tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic, as was revealed in a new book by journalist Bob Woodward, Bloomberg reported.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Wednesday it will permanently block its border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures to cope with “confrontational hysteria" by South Korean and U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: British doctor admits to attempted murder after injecting mother’s partner with poison disguised as vaccine
item.description: British doctor, Thomas Kwan, pleaded guilty to an attempted murder charge after 'overwhelming' evidence showed he administer a poisonous fake COVID vaccination.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea's Kim again threatens to use nuclear weapons against South Korea and US
item.description: The latest warning came as experts say North Korea could ramp up hostilities ahead of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: War rages on multiple fronts as Israel marks a year since Hamas' Oct. 7 attack
item.description: RE'IM, Israel (AP) — Israelis held somber ceremonies Monday to mark a year since the deadliest attack in the country’s history, a Hamas-led raid that shattered its sense of security and has since spiraled into wars on two fronts with no end in sight. Hamas marked the anniversary of its Oct.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Iran’s missiles strike Israel, and a deadly terror attack in Tel Aviv
item.description: On the same day Iran attacked Israel with some 181 missiles last week, a terrorist attack saw 7 innocent Israelis murdered in cold blood by two Palestinians in the Tel Aviv area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four American hostages remain in Hamas captivity a year after Oct. 7 massacre
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is vowing that the U.S. "will continue to work tirelessly" to bring home Americans being held hostage by Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas launches rockets from Gaza one year after Oct. 7 attacks, while IDF strikes terror targets
item.description: Hamas launched five rockets into Israel on the anniversary of the Oct. 7 terrorist attacks that started the war in Gaza, the Israel Defense Forces say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Saving lives on 'Death Street,' how an Israeli kindergarten teacher became a battlefield hero on October 7
item.description: When Hamas terrorists attacked her hometown in Southern Israel, Tali Hadad a kindergarten teacher, leapt into action to save 13 people, including her own wounded son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American father of Hamas hostage Itay Chen pushes US, Israel on ‘Plan B’ as negotiations falter
item.description: The father of an American held by Hamas is urging the Biden administration to think outside of the box and return his son and others from Gaza as Israel's year-long strategy fails to free them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli airstrikes rock southern suburbs of Beirut and cut off a key crossing into Syria
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel carried out another series of punishing airstrikes Friday, hitting suburban Beirut and cutting off the main border crossing between Lebanon and Syria for tens of thousands of people fleeing the Israeli bombardment of the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Relatives say a whole family was killed in Israel's deadliest West Bank strike since Oct. 7
item.description: TULKAREM, West Bank (AP) — An Israeli airstrike on a West Bank cafe that the military said targeted Palestinian militants also killed a family of four, including two young children, relatives told The Associated Press on Friday.
item.dc:creator: NASSER NASSER and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US arranges flights to bring Americans out of Lebanon as others seek escape
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S.-arranged flights have brought about 350 Americans and their immediate relatives out of Lebanon this week during escalated fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, while thousands of others still there face airstrikes and diminishing commercial flights.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
